title: Pemex Pipeline Leak Spills Crude into Gulf of Mexico — Three Officials Dismissed for Failure to Report
detail: A leak in a Petróleos Mexicanos (Pemex) pipeline caused a crude oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico on April 17, 2026. Three company officials were fired for failing to report the incident promptly, marking a public accountability action by the Sheinbaum administration against the state oil company. The spill follows ongoing concerns about Pemex infrastructure decay — the company carries over $100 billion in debt and has seen production declines for years. The incident comes one week after President Sheinbaum announced a fracking plan to reduce Mexico's dependence on US natural gas imports and as the administration attempts to assert greater state control over Mexico's energy sector. Environmental and maritime officials assessed the scope of contamination.

media.caption: AFP report on Pemex Gulf of Mexico pipeline leak and official dismissals
